original_abstract: A central objective of implementation science is the identification, development, and rigorous testing of implementation strategies. However, progress in this field has been frequently stymied by inconsistent terminology and poor descriptions of these strategies in existing literature. To address these challenges, the Expert Recommendations for Implementing Change (ERIC) study was designed to refine a standardized compilation of implementation strategy terms and definitions. This was achieved by systematically incorporating feedback from a diverse group of stakeholders, including both implementation science experts and clinical practitioners, to ensure the framework is both conceptually sound and practical.
humanized_abstract: One of the main goals of implementation science is the identification, development, and evaluation of implementation strategies. Work in this area has been held up by variations in terminology and inadequate description of the various strategies in the literature. The Expert Recommendations for Implementing Change (ERIC) study was conducted to enhance and refine the vocabulary and definitions of implementation terms. Through soliciting input from both implementation scientists and practice providers, ERIC has resulted in a dictionary of terms about implementation strategies that is both theoretically sound and clinically relevant.

Through soliciting input from both implementation scientists and practice providers, ERIC has resulted in a dictionary of terms about implementation strategies that is both theoretically sound and clinically relevant.
